zh.Biddenden, Ashford Borough, Kent, Englan: 英國英格蘭肯特比登登
zh.Benenden, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特貝嫩登
zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry Allarde於1565年3月25日在英國肯特比登登受洗時，他的父親Richard Allarde33歲，母親Ellen Hammone31歲。他於1586年1月9日與Phillipa Gibbon在英國英格蘭肯特貝嫩登結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他埋葬在英國肯特比登登。

zh-hans.Benenden, Kent, England: 英国英格兰肯特貝嫩登
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry Allarde于1565年3月25日在英国肯特比登登受洗时，他的父亲Richard Allarde33岁，母亲Ellen Hammone31岁。他于1586年1月9日与Phillipa Gibbon在英国英格兰肯特貝嫩登结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他埋葬在英国肯特比登登。

nameMeanings.name: Allarde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Andre, Emile, Normand, Marcel, Alain, Armand, Gaston, Pierre, Antoine, Benoit, Fernand.

English: from a Middle English personal name, Alard, Adelard, Aylard, or Elard. The former two names are usually of native English origin, from Old English Æthelheard, composed of the elements æthel ‘noble’ + heard ‘hard, bold’. Alternatively, the surname may be of continental origin, from an ancient Germanic name (see 2 below), which developed in Old French to Alard, and in Middle Dutch to Adelaert and Alaert. The continental names were used in post-Conquest England by Normans and Flemings. Later development of -ard to -ett and -att in surnames means that these personal names may also lie behind some examples of e.g. Allatt and Hallett .
French: from the medieval personal name Alard, from ancient Germanic Adelhard, composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + hard ‘hardy’. Compare Allar , Allor , Allord , and Allore .

variants.name: Ellerd
meanings: English (Bedfordshire and Northamptonshire): variant of   Allard  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.
Probably an altered form of German Ellert, a variant of Ehlert.

variants.name: Ellert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Merwin, Traude.

variants.name: Hallett
meanings: English (mainly Somerset and Dorset): variant of Allatt, itself a variant of Allard , with prosthetic H-.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Allarde was christened on 25 March 1565, in Biddenden, Kent, England, his father, Richard Allarde, was 33 and his mother, Ellen Hammone, was 31. He married Phillipa Gibbon on 9 January 1586, in Benenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He was buried in Biddenden, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry Allarde a été baptisé le 25 mars 1565, à Biddenden, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Richard Allarde, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Ellen Hammone, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Phillipa Gibbon le 9 janvier 1586, à Benenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Ilenterré à Biddenden, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henry Allarde am 25. März 1565 in Biddenden, Kent, England getauft wurde, war sein Vater Richard Allarde 33 und seine Mutter Ellen Hammone 31. Er hat am 9. Januar 1586 in Benenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom Phillipa Gibbon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er wurde in Biddenden, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Allarde è stato battezzato il 25 marzo 1565 nel Biddenden, Kent, England, il suo padre, Richard Allarde, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Ellen Hammone, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Phillipa Gibbon il 9 gennaio 1586, nel Benenden, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Fu sepolto a Biddenden, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry Allardeが1565年3月25日にイギリスケントビッドエンデンで洗礼を受けた時，父Richard Allardeは33歳，母Ellen Hammoneは31歳であった。1586年1月9日にイギリスイギリスケントベンエンデンでPhillipa Gibbonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスケントビッドエンデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry Allarde(이)가 1565년 3월 25일 영국 켄트 비데든에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Allarde(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Ellen Hammone(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1586년 1월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 베니든에서 Phillipa Gibbon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 켄트 비데든에 묻혔다.

pt.25 March 1565–15 July 1645: 25 de março de 1565–15 de julho de 1645
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Allarde foi batizado em 25 de março de 1565, em Biddenden, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Allarde, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Ellen Hammone, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Phillipa Gibbon em 9 de janeiro de 1586, em Benenden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele foi sepultado em Biddenden, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry Allarde был крещен 25 марта 1565 г. в местности бидденден, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Allarde было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Ellen Hammone, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Phillipa Gibbon 9 января 1586 г. в местности биненден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он был похоронен в местности бидденден, кэнт, Англия.

es.25 March 1565–15 July 1645: 25 de marzo de 1565–15 de julio de 1645
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry Allarde se bautizó el 25 de marzo de 1565, en Biddenden, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Allarde, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Ellen Hammone, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Phillipa Gibbon el 9 de enero de 1586, en Benenden, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Fue sepultado en Biddenden, Kent, Inglaterra.
